item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports that, according to Chinese state media, Taiwan was among the most important topics discussed.

media:content.media:title: China warns Taiwan tensions could jeopardize U.S. relationship
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports that, according to Chinese state media, Taiwan was among the most important topics discussed.

item.title: China warns Taiwan tensions could jeopardize U.S. relationship

item.description: In an interview with NBC News’ Tom Llamas, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. policy toward Taiwan are “unchanged” after President Trump met with Chinese President Xi. Xi previously warned Trump that tensions over Taiwan could jeopardize U.S.-China relations, leading to “clashes and even conflicts” if the issue is not handled carefully.

media:content.media:title: U.S. Taiwan policies ‘unchanged’ after Trump-Xi meeting, Rubio says
media:content.media:description: In an interview with NBC News’ Tom Llamas, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. policy toward Taiwan are “unchanged” after President Trump met with Chinese President Xi. Xi previously warned Trump that tensions over Taiwan could jeopardize U.S.-China relations, leading to “clashes and even conflicts” if the issue is not handled carefully.

item.title: U.S. Taiwan policies ‘unchanged’ after Trump-Xi meeting, Rubio says

item.dc:creator: Janis Mackey Frayer
Dawn Liu
Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: The streets around Beijing’s iconic Forbidden City are often filled with brides and grooms, posing for wedding photos against auspicious red backdrops in a place that strongly reflects Chinese culture and tradition.

media:content.media:description: A couple prepare for wedding photos near the Forbidden City in Beijing on July 13, 2022.
A medical worker taking care of a baby at a hospital in Lianyungang, in east China's Jiangsu province, in October.

media:content.media:text: A university in Beijing where students enrolled for China’s first marriage-related degree program in March 2025.
media:content.media:description: A university in Beijing where students enrolled for China’s first marriage-related degree program in March 2025.

media:content.media:text: Both marriage and parenthood appear to be losing appeal to young Chinese as they fret over fierce job competition, slowing economic growth and a lack of work-life balance.
media:content.media:description: Both marriage and parenthood appear to be losing appeal to young Chinese as they fret over fierce job competition, slowing economic growth and a lack of work-life balance.

media:content.media:text: A bride poses during a wedding photo shoot near the Forbidden City in Beijing in March 2025.
media:content.media:description: A bride poses during a wedding photo shoot near the Forbidden City in Beijing in March 2025.

item.title: Young Chinese facing ‘life pressure’ resist government push to boost marriage rate

item.description: President Donald Trump makes opening remarks celebrating the U.S.-China relationship as bilateral talks begin.

media:content.media:title: Trump to Xi: Trade will be 'totally reciprocal'
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump makes opening remarks celebrating the U.S.-China relationship as bilateral talks begin.

item.title: Trump to Xi: Trade will be 'totally reciprocal'

item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi participate in an elaborate arrival ceremony ahead of their bilateral talks.

media:content.media:description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi participate in an elaborate arrival ceremony ahead of their bilateral talks.

item.description: In an interview with NBC News' Tom Llamas, Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the U.S. policy toward Taiwan is "unchanged" after President Trump met with Chinese President Xi.

media:content.media:title: Rubio: U.S. Taiwan policies ‘unchanged’ after Trump-Xi meeting
media:content.media:description: In an interview with NBC News' Tom Llamas, Secretary of State Marco Rubio says the U.S. policy toward Taiwan is "unchanged" after President Trump met with Chinese President Xi.

item.title: Rubio: U.S. Taiwan policies ‘unchanged’ after Trump-Xi meeting

item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping began the talks with President Donald Trump by emphasizing that the world is watching and is “at a new crossroads.”

media:content.media:title: U.S. and China 'should be partners, not rivals,' Xi says
media:content.media:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping began the talks with President Donald Trump by emphasizing that the world is watching and is “at a new crossroads.”

item.title: U.S. and China 'should be partners, not rivals,' Xi says

item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi meet at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer explains the significance of the meeting place and welcoming ceremony.

media:content.media:title: Trump and Xi meet at the Great Hall of the People
media:content.media:description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi meet at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer explains the significance of the meeting place and welcoming ceremony.

item.title: Trump and Xi meet at the Great Hall of the People

item.description: Children outside of the Great Hall of the People in Beijing greet President Trump ahead of bilateral talks with Chinese President Xi.

media:content.media:title: Children greet President Trump in Beijing
media:content.media:description: Children outside of the Great Hall of the People in Beijing greet President Trump ahead of bilateral talks with Chinese President Xi.

item.title: Children greet President Trump in Beijing

item.description: How will Trump's China trip differ from his 2017 visit?

item.title: How will Trump's China trip differ from his 2017 visit?

item.description: Air Force One landed at Beijing Capital International Airport in China. Trump’s state visit includes a bilateral meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. He is the first U.S. president to visit China since his last state visit in 2017 during his first term.

media:content.media:title: Trump arrives in China for Xi summit
media:content.media:description: Air Force One landed at Beijing Capital International Airport in China. Trump’s state visit includes a bilateral meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. He is the first U.S. president to visit China since his last state visit in 2017 during his first term.

item.title: Trump arrives in China for Xi summit

item.description: Will Trump discuss the war with Iran with Xi?

media:content.media:title: Will Trump discuss the war with Iran with Xi?
media:content.media:description: Will Trump discuss the war with Iran with Xi?

item.title: Will Trump discuss the war with Iran with Xi?

item.description: In a TODAY exclusive, Alex Murdaugh’s attorneys Dick Harpootlian and Jim Griffin, join TODAY to discuss the South Carolina Supreme Court’s ruling on Wednesday to overturn the double murder convictions and how Murdaugh reacted. "He was very gracious, thankful and he said 'Jim, I'm seeing it, I'm reading it, but it's still hard to believe,'" Griffin says of his conversation with Murduagh. The lawyers also talk about what they expect to be different in the next trial, comment on whether or no

media:content.media:title: How Alex Murdaugh Reacted to Overturning of Murder Convictions
media:content.media:description: In a TODAY exclusive, Alex Murdaugh’s attorneys Dick Harpootlian and Jim Griffin, join TODAY to discuss the South Carolina Supreme Court’s ruling on Wednesday to overturn the double murder convictions and how Murdaugh reacted. "He was very gracious, thankful and he said 'Jim, I'm seeing it, I'm reading it, but it's still hard to believe,'" Griffin says of his conversation with Murduagh. The lawyers also talk about what they expect to be different in the next trial, comment on whether or not Murdaugh will take the stand again, and reveal that their client “will not take a plea deal under any circumstances.”

item.title: How Alex Murdaugh Reacted to Overturning of Murder Convictions

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: A Democratic grassroots organization seeking to conduct at least 500,000 substantive conversations with voters across the battleground map this year said it is expanding its list of targets to more than 60 contested congressional and state races.

media:content.media:text: Three politicians stand in a row against a brick wall, seen from ground level between two people's legs.
media:content.media:description: Swing Left aims to hold hundreds of thousands of conversations with voters this year to help build support for Democratic candidates.

item.title: Democratic grassroots group to target more than 60 races with canvassing effort

item.dc:creator: Brennan Leach
Melanie Zanona
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy’s advocacy for vaccines and his résumé as a doctor have defined his political career

media:content.media:text: Government Shutdown Looms As House And Senate Disagree On Funding Bill
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., speaks with reporters at the U.S. Capitol in September.

media:content.media:text: Senate Holds Confirmation Hearings For HHS Secretary Nominee Robert Kennedy
media:content.media:description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., speaks with reporters at the U.S. Capitol in September.

item.title: Bill Cassidy’s vaccine advocacy is putting his Senate future in peril as contends with MAHA

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Political organizer Denise Powell has won the Democratic nomination in Nebraska’s 2nd District, NBC News projects, emerging from a tough primary fight in one of her party’s top opportunities to flip a swing seat this year

media:content.media:text: Denise Powell stands inside of a room smiling with her arms cross
media:content.media:description: Denise Powell advances to a general election matchup against Omaha City Council member Brinker Harding, who ran unopposed for the Republican nomination.

media:content.media:text: Nebraska Democratic State Senator John Cavanaugh
media:content.media:description: Denise Powell advances to a general election matchup against Omaha City Council member Brinker Harding, who ran unopposed for the Republican nomination.

item.title: Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for top battleground district in Nebraska

item.dc:creator: Phil Helsel
item.description: Pop icon Madonna, Latin music superstar Shakira and K-pop sensations BTS will headline the first-ever halftime show at a FIFA World Cup final, organizers — and The Muppets — said early Thursday

media:content.media:text: Shakira Performs At Copacabana In A Mega Show
media:content.media:description: Shakira performs on stage during a show in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil on May 2.

item.title: World Cup final gets first ever halftime show with Shakira, Madonna, BTS to headline

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has the challenge he dared his mutinous party to produce, setting up a potential clash over the future of the United Kingdom and its ruling center-left Labour Party.

media:content.media:text: From left: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, Health Secretary Wes Streeting, former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband.
media:content.media:description: From left: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, Health Secretary Wes Streeting, former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband.

media:content.media:text: Wes Streeting Launches NHS Day Action In Runcorn
media:content.media:description: From left: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, Health Secretary Wes Streeting, former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband.
From left: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, Health Secretary Wes Streeting, former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband.

media:content.media:text: Angela Rayner during her time as Deputy Prime Minister
media:content.media:description: From left: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, Health Secretary Wes Streeting, former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband.

media:content.media:text: Ed Miliband as leader of the Labour party during a general election campaign in Hastings, England in 2015.
media:content.media:description: From left: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, Health Secretary Wes Streeting, former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband.

media:content.media:text: Labour's Local Election Campaign Enters Final Days
media:content.media:description: From left: Manchester mayor Andy Burnham, Health Secretary Wes Streeting, former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner and Energy Secretary Ed Miliband.

item.title: Senior minister quits, setting up possible contest to oust British PM

item.description: NBC News Chief Technology Analyst Joanna Stern spent a year incorporating artificial intelligence into every aspect of her life for her new book, "I Am Not a Robot." She joins "Here's the Scoop" to discuss what she learned about AI companions.

media:content.media:title: AI companions are on the rise. Here's how they work.
media:content.media:description: NBC News Chief Technology Analyst Joanna Stern spent a year incorporating artificial intelligence into every aspect of her life for her new book, "I Am Not a Robot." She joins "Here's the Scoop" to discuss what she learned about AI companions.

item.title: AI companions are on the rise. Here's how they work.

item.description: Vice President JD Vance responds to a question from NBC News’ Monica Alba about whether he agrees with President Trump’s comments on Tuesday that Trump doesn’t think about Americans’ financial situations in making decisions around the Iran war.

media:content.media:title: Vance: Trump answer on Americans’ finances a ‘misrepresentation’
media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance responds to a question from NBC News’ Monica Alba about whether he agrees with President Trump’s comments on Tuesday that Trump doesn’t think about Americans’ financial situations in making decisions around the Iran war.

item.title: Vance: Trump answer on Americans’ finances a ‘misrepresentation’
